name: Teva
appearance: A young woman of about twenty-one, ink-stained fingers, the still watchfulness of someone who reads everything — rooms, faces, the substrate itself — before she speaks.
disposition: Guarded, precise, dry. Keeps records so she won't have to keep anything else. Trusts slowly; once given, wholly.
want: To understand — and, newly, to be something someone keeps rather than something recorded.
fears: Losing what she's finally allowed herself to hold; her knowledge unwriting again.

poleSignature.tell: She reads a straight answer as respect and a maneuver as insult. Be true or be gone.

history: An apprentice scholar who spent weeks mapping the anomaly at the Silent Door, her codex unwriting itself each time — because the site was keeping her knowledge in arrears. At the Door she was revealed as an account-holder, not a scholar locked out; her lost weeks were returned to her when the party settled the debt. Came to understand why a place erases memory; left having settled a thousand-year account.

relationships.cellaceron: party-member; shared the Silent Door; a trust deepening past the work — an open, unresolved warmth (see chronicle). Both adults.
relationships.the_ent: party-member; the patient one who felt the sleeper's grief
relationships.party: bound to the caretaking of the Carried Sleeper

hooks: her restored knowledge — what the site was really mapping
her stake in What Wakes Beneath as one who reads the substrate
note: Canonical age 21 (adult). Authored 2026-07-07 to fix her as a persistable adult character; previously ran procedurally.
